codeReview.summary: The fix removes the flaky idempotent false-negative but does so by permitting a backward tolerance window, which breaks strict monotonic semantics required by the feature contract.

issues.description: The monotonic guard allows `newUpperBound.timeIntervalSince(currentBound) >= -0.001`, so genuine backward advances up to 1ms are accepted. The expected behavior requires idempotent repeats to pass while actual backward moves are still rejected.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The indexing-efficiency-worker procedure appears over-broad for narrowly scoped app-layer backfill fixes, causing repeated procedure drift in validator evidence.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Skill step 6 requires `swift test --package-path OpenBurnBarCore` (and retrieval evals when relevant), while this worker's transcript/handoff only shows scoped Xcode BackfillScheduler test commands.

summary: Fail. Commit ae68a630f8999a62eec523b99a49bec6203a1613 widens monotonic epsilon to 1ms, which fixes the flaky idempotent check but also admits small true backward cursor movement, so it does not fully satisfy the required monotonic/backward semantics.
